stories.title: As fuel prices continue to rise, Iran war drives Europe toward new crisis
stories.description: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.

stories.title: IMF predicts Iran war will slow economic growth and raise inflation globally
stories.description: The International Monetary Fund expects the Iran war to slow the global economy and raise inflation. NPR's Michel Martin discusses the forecast with Simon Johnson, a former IMF chief economist.

stories.title: Israel is building a buffer zone inside Lebanon
stories.description: Israel is creating a large buffer zone in southern Lebanon for a prolonged military occupation, with low expectations that direct talks with Lebanon will lead to quick action on disarming Hezbollah.

stories.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
stories.description: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.

stories.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
stories.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

stories.title: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.description: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
stories.description: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.description: The Lebanese government go into peace talks with limited influence over the group.

stories.title: US blockade of Iran ports irresponsible and dangerous, China says
stories.description: China, the biggest buyer of Iranian oil, says the US naval blockade undermines an "already fragile ceasefire".

stories.title: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests
stories.description: The vast majority did not believe Hezbollah or Iran had been severely weakened by weeks of Israeli and US attacks, according to the poll.

stories.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
stories.description: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

stories.title: Israel steps up strikes on Lebanon and Hezbollah attacks continue as talks loom
stories.description: More than 160 people have been killed since Friday in Lebanon, where Israel is pressing on with its campaign against Hezbollah despite the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Starmer says UK will not join Trump's blockade of Iran's ports
stories.description: UK minesweepers and anti-drone capabilities will continue operating in the region, as pressure ramps up to reopen the key shipping route.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: War and Peace: Israel brings Lebanon to negotiating table as airstrikes rain down on Beirut
stories.description: François Picard welcomes Farès Boueiz, former Lebanese Minister of Foreign Affairs. The Israeli-Lebanese talks in the US cannot be defined as a "breakthrough", explains the former Lebanese minister. He sees this as a technical prelude to meaningful negotiations. stories.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to direct negotiations following talks in Washington on Tuesday that US Secretary of State Marco Rubio had hailed as a "historic opportunity" for peace. Lebanon's ambassador to the United States Nada Hamadeh Moawad called for a ceasefire. FRANCE 24's Cyr...

stories.title: France and UK to co-host Hormuz shipping security talks
stories.description: France and Britain will co-host a video-conference Friday of countries ready to contribute to a "purely defensive mission" to secure the Strait of Hormuz, the French president's office said Tuesday. President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer are to co-chair...

stories.title: Israeli army issues new evacuation call to south Lebanon residents
stories.description: Israel's military on Wednesday issued a fresh evacuation order for residents of south Lebanon as it pressed on with an offensive against Hezbollah militants. It is the first evacuation order by the military since direct talks were held on Tuesday in Washington between Lebanon and...

stories.title: US urges IMF, World Bank to abandon climate finance goals
stories.description: The climate-denialist Trump administration is pushing the World Bank and International Monetary Fund to stop focusing on clean energy development projects. We take a closer look. But first: Russia vows to make up for any shortfall in Chinese oil supplies due to the US-Israeli war...

stories.title: Ambassadors meet for historic talks in US amid deeply fraught Lebanon-Israel tensions
stories.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Simon Mabon, Professor of International Politics and Director of the Richardson Institute for Peace Studies at Lancaster University. He sees the current conflict between Lebanon and Israel as one defined more by layered, overlapping sovereignties....

stories.title: Middle East live: Israeli army targets vehicles south of Beirut, Hezbollah responds with rockets
stories.description: Israel’s army on Wednesday struck two vehicles south of Beirut, while the Hezbollah militant group responded by sending a barrage of rockets towards Israel. The attacks come just hours after Lebanon and Israel met in Washington, where they agreed to hold direct talks. Follow our ...

stories.title: Iran citizen held in France over pro-Palestine comments returns home
stories.description: Release of Mahdieh Esfandiari comes a week after Iran released two French citizens held on espionage charges.

stories.title: ‘Food is unifying’: How Lebanon’s migrants are feeding the war-displaced
stories.description: Often overlooked in Lebanon's society, the migrant community is filling the gaps of the state by feeding their own.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 47 of the US-Iran conflict?
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has signalled a possible second round of talks with Iran in the coming days.

stories.title: Trump turns on Meloni, saying she lacks ‘courage’ over US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: The US president says he is 'shocked at her', delivering a blunt public rebuke to one of his closest European allies.

stories.title: China’s Xi meets Russian FM Lavrov, calls relations with Moscow ‘precious’
stories.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov met with top Chinese leadership to discuss the Iran war and other concerns.

stories.title: Iran says $270bn war loss must be compensated, as fresh talks with US loom
stories.description: Iran demands compensation for war damages caused by US and Israeli attacks on critical infrastructure.

stories.title: US military says blockade of Iran ports ‘completely’ halts economic trade
stories.description: US Central Command say more than 10,000 armed forces members, as well as warships and planes, are involved in blockade.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon hold direct talks for first time in decades
stories.description: After the first direct talks in decades, Israel and Lebanon have agreed to begin negotiations.

stories.title: Xi meets Russian FM as leaders flock to China over Middle East war
stories.description: China’s President Xi Jinping met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov on Wednesday, Chinese state media said, as a string of leaders of countries impacted by the Middle East war flock to Beijing. stories.title: Trump doubles down in criticising Pope Leo over Iran
stories.description: US President Donald Trump, whose war and immigration policies have been condemned by Pope Leo, reiterated his criticism of the religious leader on Tuesday night. In a post on Truth Social, Trump urged that “someone please tell Pope Leo” about the killings of protesters by Iran an...

stories.title: US hosts ‘rare’ direct talks between Lebanon and Israel
stories.description: • European nations, Rubio urge both sides to ‘seize the opportunity’ to end conflict• Joint statement says direct negotiations can pave way for lasting security• Lebanese president hopes meeting will ‘mark the beginning of ending the suffering’ as Beirut seeks ceasefire WASHINGTO...

stories.title: King Charles to address Congress on US trip marking ‘shared history’: palace
stories.description: King Charles III will give a landmark address to the US Congress on his first state visit this month, marking “shared history” and deep ties, Buckingham Palace said Tuesday. Making his first state visit to the US as monarch from April 27, Charles will address lawmakers from both ...

stories.title: China, Spain call for closer ties amid ‘crumbling’ world order
stories.description: BEIJING: Europe and China must forge closer ties to counter threats to multilateralism, Spanish Premier Pedro Sanchez said on Tuesday after meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping, who said the international order is “crumbling”. Sanchez’s April 11 to 15 visit to China comes as many...

stories.title: Pope walks in Augustine’s footsteps on Algeria trip
stories.description: ANNABA: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Tuesday in the Algerian city of Annaba, the birthplace of Christian theologian Saint Augustine, on day two of a historic trip marred by two suicide attacks. While the authorities have yet to comment, an informed source on Tuesday confirmed the bomb...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: Trump not considering extending ceasefire with Iran, says 'deal is preferable'
stories.description: Trump expressed confidence about imminent developments, saying, "I think you're going to be watching an amazing two days ahead. I really do."

stories.title: Children killed in Lebanon as Israeli strikes hit homes far from front lines of war with Hezbollah
stories.description: “Israeli military didn't deny that children have been killed in its Lebanon strikes but said it has targeted Hezbollah facilities and militants,” media report

stories.title: Jaishankar speaks to Israeli counterpart Gideon Sa'ar over ‘different aspects’ of West Asia crisis
stories.description: The phone conversation between the two foreign ministers came amid reports of efforts by the U.S. and Iran to hold a fresh round of negotiations after the collapse of the initial dialogue in Islamabad

stories.description: Officials from Pakistan, Iran and the Gulf also said negotiating teams from the U.S. and Iran could ‌return ⁠to Pakistan later this week

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 stranded Iranian sailors: Minister
stories.description: Deputy Defence Minister Aruna Jayasekara said 32 sailors rescued from the IRIS Dena — a frigate attacked on March 4 just off Sri Lanka — and another 206 from the IRIS Bushehr left on April 14, 2026

stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced the blockade on Sunday (April 12, 2026) after weekend ‌peace talks in Islamabad between the U.S. and Iran failed to reach a deal

stories.description: According to an analytical report, Iran has already vowed to attack US ships with small speed boats and inexpensive drones
